context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Marcus Johnson  
- **Age:** 32  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — 4 years total training, ~2.5 years focused powerlifting; competed (USAPL local meet, Nov 2024) placing **3rd** in **220 lb class** with **8/9** successful lifts.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** Peak for upcoming powerlifting meet in **~12 weeks (as of 2026-02-27)**  
  - **Increase total:** **1350 lb → 1450 lb**  
  - **Squat:** **455 → 485 lb**  
  - **Bench:** **315 → 340 lb**  
  - **Deadlift:** **580 → 625 lb**
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Fix **bench sticking point ~2–3 inches off chest**
  - Improve **squat depth consistency** to meet standards (competition-quality depth every rep)
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **Powerlifting meet:** ~12 weeks out (**date not provided**)

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Tue / Thu / Fri**
- **Desired training frequency:** **4x/week**
- **Typical time window:** **5:30 AM** (session duration not provided)

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Commercial gym — **Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym (Tempe, AZ)**
- **Available equipment (notable):**
  - Competition-spec **power racks**, **monolifts**
  - Specialty bars: **SSB**, **Swiss bar**, **Texas deadlift bar**
  - **Bands**, **chains**
  - Dumbbells up to **150 lb**
  - **GHD**, **reverse hyper**
  - Standard barbells/plates (implied by facility)

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Chronic **right shoulder impingement** — aggravated by **overhead work** and **wide-grip bench**; cannot do **strict overhead press**; uses **landmine** variations; managed with **daily band work**; sees **sports PT monthly** *(since 2026-02-27; ongoing—monitor and reassess)*

#### Preferences
- Programming: **percentage-based**, likes **DUP**, familiar with and can use **RPE**
- Movement modifications: avoids strict OHP; prefers **landmine pressing**; likely better tolerance with non-wide grips on bench (based on constraint)
- Competitive focus: powerlifting meet prep / specificity prioritized

---

### METRICS
*(Powerlifting prioritized)*

**Strength (Powerlifting)**
- **Current total:** **1350 lb** *(as of 2026-02-27; source: user report)*
- **Squat:** **455 lb** *(as of 2026-02-27; current level per goal baseline)*
- **Bench Press:** **315 lb** *(as of 2026-02-27; current level per goal baseline)*
- **Deadlift:** **580 lb** *(as of 2026-02-27; current level per goal baseline)*
- **Meet history:** USAPL local meet **Nov 2024** — **8/9**, **3rd place**, **220 lb class** *(exact meet lifts not provided)*

**Movement Quality**
- **Bench:** sticking point **2–3 inches off chest** *(as of 2026-02-27)*
- **Squat:** depth consistency needs improvement *(as of 2026-02-27)*
- **Shoulder:** overhead pressing intolerance due to impingement *(as of 2026-02-27)*

**Body Composition**
- Weight class reference: **220 lb class (USAPL)** *(as of Nov 2024; current bodyweight not provided)*

---

### LOG

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (meet prep intake)
- Set primary objective: peak for meet in ~12 weeks; target total **1450 lb** from current **1350 lb**.
- Captured lift targets: SQ **455→485**, BP **315→340**, DL **580→625**.
- Noted technical/performance priorities: bench mid-range (2–3" off chest) and squat depth consistency.
- Training schedule confirmed: **Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri at 5:30 AM**; prefers **percentage-based/DUP**, can use **RPE**.
- Equipment confirmed: Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym (Tempe, AZ) with specialty bars, bands/chains, monolifts, reverse hyper, GHD, DBs to 150.
- Constraint added: **[ACTIVE]** chronic right shoulder impingement; avoid strict OHP and wide-grip bench; uses landmine work; monthly PT.

**Missing data to complete/validate further (optional to provide next):**
- Meet **date**, federation (assumed USAPL), and intended **weight class** for upcoming meet
- Current **bodyweight** (and trend) + any weigh-in strategy
- Recent e1RM/rep PRs with dates, and most recent training block structure (DUP split, key variations)
- Any competition commands/standards issues (pause bench, squat depth calls, lockout) from Nov 2024 meet feedback</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program Name:** 12-Week Powerlifting Meet Peak — Depth-Consistent Squat + Mid-Range Bench Breakthrough  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** Marcus Johnson  
- **Goal:** Peak for powerlifting meet in ~12 weeks; increase total **1350 → 1450** while improving squat depth consistency and bench strength **2–3" off chest**  
- **Duration:** **12 weeks (meet prep)**

---

## Program Philosophy
Marcus is an intermediate powerlifter with meet experience and specific performance targets, so this plan uses **competition-lift specificity**, **DUP (daily undulating periodization)**, and **percentage-based loading** with RPE guardrails to peak reliably in 12 weeks. The program emphasizes **3 bench exposures/week** (to drive bench progress and address the off-chest sticking region) and **2 squat + 2 deadlift exposures/week** (enough frequency to build skill and strength without burying recovery in a 4-day schedule). To improve **squat depth consistency**, the plan includes **paused/tempo squat work early**, then transitions toward more competition singles as the meet approaches. The active right-shoulder impingement is managed by **avoiding strict overhead press and wide-grip bench**, prioritizing **shoulder-friendly pressing variants (Spoto, close-to-moderate grip, Swiss-bar/DB where needed, landmine)**, and keeping **upper-back/rear-delt/rotator cuff** work consistent. Progression is primarily **% of Training Max (TM)** with **weekly intensity rises and planned deloads**, while using **RPE** to cap “bad-day” overload and protect the shoulder.

**Set your Training Maxes (TM) before Week 1 (recommended):**  
- Use **TM = ~90–92.5% of current true 1RM** (or best gym single in last 4–6 weeks).  
- If using current numbers as true max: SQ 455 / BP 315 / DL 580 → starting TMs roughly **SQ 420**, **BP 290**, **DL 535** (adjust if recent singles suggest otherwise).

---

# Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): Accumulation + Technical Foundation (Depth & Off-Chest)
**Intent:** Build work capacity and tighten competition standards. Emphasis on **paused squat for depth**, and **Spoto/paused bench** for mid-range control and power.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Squat Heavy + Bench Volume**
- **Focus:** Squat competition pattern + bench volume without shoulder flare-ups  
- **Volume:** ~18–26 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** 1 × 1 @ 75–80% TM (RPE 6–7), then 4 × 5 @ 70–75%  
  *Practice depth to meet standard on every rep; single builds setup consistency without fatigue.*
- **Paused Squat (2-count in the hole):** 3 × 3 @ 60–70%  
  *Reinforces depth, bracing, and staying tight at/below parallel.*
- **Bench Press (competition grip—avoid wide):** 5 × 6 @ 67–72% (RPE 6–7)  
- **Chest-Supported Row (machine or DB):** 4 × 8–12  
  *Upper-back strength supports pressing stability and shoulder position.*
- **Reverse Hyper:** 3 × 10–15 (smooth)

**Tue — Bench Heavy + Deadlift Technique**
- **Focus:** Bench intensity + deadlift positions/speed  
- **Volume:** ~16–24 working sets
- **Bench Press (comp pause):** 1 × 1 @ 80–85% (RPE 7–8), then 4 × 4 @ 75–80%  
- **Spoto Press (pause 1–2" off chest):** 3 × 5 @ 65–72%  
  *Directly targets your 2–3" off-chest sticking point by loading that exact region.*
- **Deadlift (Texas deadlift bar if used in meet prep consistently):** 6 × 2 @ 65–72% (speed focus, perfect start position)  
- **Lat Pulldown / Neutral-Grip Pull-up:** 4 × 6–10  
- **Shoulder Prehab (choose 2):** band external rotations 2 × 15–25, face pulls 2 × 15–25, scap push-ups 2 × 10–15  
  *Keeps pressing frequency tolerable with impingement history.*

**Thu — Squat Volume + Bench Technique**
- **Focus:** Squat volume with depth; bench pause quality  
- **Volume:** ~18–28 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** 5 × 4 @ 70–77% (RPE 6.5–7.5)  
- **Tempo Squat (3 sec down):** 3 × 3 @ 55–65%  
  *Improves control into depth and reduces “cutting high.”*
- **Bench Press (long pause / meet commands practice):** 6 × 3 @ 70–77% (all paused)  
- **1-Arm Cable Row (controlled):** 3 × 10–12/side  
- **GHD Sit-Up or Cable Crunch:** 3 × 8–15

**Fri — Deadlift Heavy + Bench Assistance (Shoulder-Friendly)**
- **Focus:** Deadlift intensity + bench mid-range reinforcement without overhead work  
- **Volume:** ~16–24 working sets
- **Deadlift:** 1 × 1 @ 80–85% (RPE 7–8), then 4 × 3 @ 75–80%  
- **Deadlift Variation (pick based on weakness):**  
  - If off-floor: **1" Deficit Deadlift** 3 × 4 @ 65–72%  
  - If lockout: **Block Pull (mid-shin)** 3 × 4 @ 70–77%  
  *Variation builds weak range without over-fatiguing the competition pull.*
- **Close-Grip Bench (moderate close, shoulder-comfortable):** 4 × 6 @ 65–72%  
- **Rear Delt Raise (machine or DB):** 3 × 12–20  
- **Hamstring Curl:** 3 × 8–15

### Phase 1 Progression Strategy (Weeks 1–4)
- **Week 1:** Start at the low end of % ranges; keep most work **RPE 6–7**.  
- **Week 2:** Add **+2.5%** to primary lift back-off work or **+1 rep per set** where prescribed ranges allow.  
- **Week 3:** Add another **+2.5%** (or small load jump) and allow top singles to reach **RPE 7.5–8** if shoulder/back feel good.  
- **Week 4 (Deload):** Reduce volume to **~50–60% of Week 3 sets**, keep intensity **~80–85% of Week 3 loads** (no grinders; top singles optional at RPE 6–7).  
- **Advance to Phase 2** if: squat depth is consistent in training and you’re hitting prescribed singles without form breakdown.

---

# Phase 2 (Weeks 5–8): Intensification (Strength Priority, Specificity Rises)
**Intent:** Shift from “building” to “demonstrating” strength: fewer reps, heavier loads, and more competition-standard exposure. Bench keeps targeted mid-range work but with more competition-style heavy paused reps.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Squat Heavy + Bench Volume (Reduced)**
- **Focus:** Squat strength + bench maintenance volume  
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** 1 × 1 @ 82–88% (RPE 7–8), then 4 × 3 @ 77–83%  
- **Paused Squat:** 3 × 2 @ 70–77%  
  *Keeps depth honest as intensities climb.*
- **Bench Press:** 4 × 5 @ 70–75%  
- **Chest-Supported Row:** 4 × 6–10  
- **Reverse Hyper:** 2–3 × 10–15

**Tue — Bench Heavy (Priority Day) + Deadlift Speed**
- **Focus:** Bench strength/paused execution + deadlift speed  
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- **Bench Press (comp pause):** 1 × 1 @ 85–90% (RPE 8–9), then 5 × 2 @ 80–85%  
- **Pin Press (pins set ~2–3" off chest) OR Spoto (alternate weekly):** 3 × 3 @ 75–82% (of bench TM)  
  *Overloads your exact sticking region while still staying shoulder-friendly.*
- **Deadlift:** 5 × 2 @ 70–75% (fast, consistent wedges)  
- **Neutral-Grip Pulldown/Pull-up:** 4 × 6–10  
- **Shoulder Prehab (choose 2):** 2–3 sets each (as in Phase 1)

**Thu — Squat Secondary + Bench Technique**
- **Focus:** Squat volume at moderate intensity + bench command practice  
- **Volume:** ~16–24 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** 4 × 4 @ 72–78% (or 5 × 3 @ 75–82% depending on fatigue)  
- **Squat Walkouts (optional):** 3 × 10–15 sec @ 95–105% (only if they help confidence)  
  *Builds unrack/brace confidence without adding lots of fatigue.*
- **Bench Press (paused):** 6 × 2 @ 75–82% (crisp pauses)  
- **Row (cable or machine):** 3 × 8–12  
- **Core:** 3 × 8–15

**Fri — Deadlift Heavy + Bench Assistance**
- **Focus:** Deadlift strength + bench supportive work  
- **Volume:** ~15–22 working sets
- **Deadlift:** 1 × 1 @ 85–90% (RPE 8–9), then 4 × 2 @ 80–85%  
- **Deadlift Variation (light):** RDL 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
  *Builds posterior chain without disrupting recovery from heavier pulls.*
- **Close-Grip Bench OR Swiss-Bar Bench (shoulder-dependent):** 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Rear Delts + Rotator Cuff:** 3 × 12–20 + 2 × 15–25

### Phase 2 Progression Strategy (Weeks 5–8)
- **Weeks 5–6:** Increase top single intensity gradually (add **+2.5%** week-to-week if reps are clean). Back-offs progress by **small load jumps** while keeping bar speed acceptable.  
- **Week 7:** Highest loading of the phase; allow top singles to touch **RPE 9** (no misses).  
- **Week 8 (Deload/Resensitization):** Cut volume **~50%**, keep a few singles around **80–85%** (RPE 6–7).  
- **Advance to Phase 3** when your paused bench singles are stable and squat depth is automatic under heavier loads.

---

# Phase 3 (Weeks 9–12): Realization / Peak + Taper (Meet Readiness)
**Intent:** Convert strength to platform performance: more singles, less fatigue, and direct work toward **openers/second attempts**. Accessories drop to maintenance; shoulder stays calm.

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Squat Peak Singles + Light Bench**
- **Focus:** Squat specificity + minimal bench volume  
- **Volume:** ~10–16 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** work to 3–5 singles @ 85–92% (RPE 7.5–9 across the session), then 2 × 2 @ ~80%  
  *Practice meet commands, depth, and tightness under near-attempt loads.*
- **Paused Squat (very light):** 2 × 2 @ 60–65% (optional if depth needs reinforcement)  
- **Bench Press (paused):** 3 × 3 @ 70–75% (easy, crisp)

**Tue — Bench Peak Singles + Deadlift Light**
- **Focus:** Bench priority; keep deadlift neural touch without fatigue  
- **Volume:** ~10–16 working sets
- **Bench Press (comp pause):** work to 4–6 singles @ 87–94% (RPE 8–9; no grinders)  
- **Pin/Spoto (light):** 2 × 3 @ ~70–75% (or skip if shoulder irritated)  
- **Deadlift:** 3 × 2 @ 65–70% (speed/position)  
- **Row/Pulldown:** 3 × 6–10

**Thu — Squat Technique + Bench Commands**
- **Focus:** Skill/commands, low fatigue  
- **Volume:** ~8–14 working sets
- **Competition Squat:** 3 × 2 @ 75–80% (beautiful reps to depth)  
- **Bench Press (pause + commands):** 5–6 singles @ 80–87% (RPE 7–8)  
- **Shoulder Prehab:** 10 minutes (band work, scap control)

**Fri — Deadlift Peak Singles + Very Light Bench**
- **Focus:** Deadlift readiness; keep bench groove  
- **Volume:** ~8–14 working sets
- **Deadlift:** work to 3–5 singles @ 87–95% (RPE 8–9), then 1–2 × 2 @ ~80% if bar speed is good  
- **Bench Press (very light):** 2 × 3 @ 65–70% (optional)  
- **Reverse Hyper or Easy Ham Curl:** 2 × 10–15 (pump only)

### Phase 3 Progression Strategy (Weeks 9–12)
- **Week 9:** Singles in the high-80s/low-90s; focus on consistency and confidence.  
- **Week 10:** “Second attempt feel” week—touch heavier singles if bar speed is there (still no grinders).  
- **Week 11:** **Openers practice** (see below). Keep volume low; everything should move fast and confident.  
- **Week 12 (Taper / Meet Week):**
  - Early week: brief sessions with **very low volume** (e.g., 3–5 singles @ 70–80% on squat/bench, 2–3 singles @ 65–75% deadlift).  
  - **No hard accessories**, prioritize sleep, shoulder feels-good work, and mobility.  
  - Last heavy pull should be **~7–10 days out** (no max deadlift close to meet day).

**Opener/Attempt Selection (guideline)**
- **Opener:** something you can triple on any day (roughly **88–92%**).  
- **Second:** near current best / confident PR range (**94–98%**).  
- **Third:** based on second attempt speed and goal total (**PR attempt** if there).  
(We’ll refine once you report Week 10–11 singles.)

---

## Phase Cycling (After the Meet)
**Order (repeatable):**  
**Accumulation/Technique (4 wks) → Intensification (4 wks) → Realization/Peak (3–4 wks) → Post-meet recovery (1–2 wks) → repeat**

**Transition triggers:**
- Move from Accumulation → Intensification when technique is stable and soreness is manageable week-to-week.
- Move from Intensification → Peak when heavy singles are consistent and you’re not accumulating joint irritation (especially shoulder).
- After meet: take **7–14 days** of reduced loading and higher-rep hypertrophy work, then re-test TMs and start a new cycle.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial 12-week meet-prep program created based on 4-day schedule, shoulder impingement constraint (no strict OHP, avoid wide grip), bench mid-range sticking point, and squat depth consistency goal.

--- 

If you tell me (1) the exact meet date, (2) whether you’ll pull **Texas deadlift bar** in the meet or only in training, and (3) your preferred squat setup (high vs low bar + stance), I can lock in opener week specifics and fine-tune the variation choices (pins vs Spoto rotation, deficit vs block pull choice) even tighter.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** 12-Week Powerlifting Meet Peak — Depth-Consistent Squat + Mid-Range Bench Breakthrough  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Accumulation + Technical Foundation (Week 1 / 12)  
**User:** Marcus Johnson  

## Schedule
- **Mon (5:30 AM):** Squat Heavy + Bench Volume (Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym)
- **Tue (5:30 AM):** Bench Heavy + Deadlift Technique (Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym)
- **Wed:** Rest
- **Thu (5:30 AM):** Squat Volume + Bench Technique (Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym)
- **Fri (5:30 AM):** Deadlift Heavy + Bench Assistance (Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym)
- **Sat:** Rest / optional easy walk + shoulder band work
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 of Phase 1 = conservative start: stay mostly **RPE 6–7**, nail competition standards, and leave reps in the tank. Squat work emphasizes **depth consistency** (paused + tempo), and bench includes **Spoto/long pauses** to attack the **2–3" off-chest** sticking point while keeping your right shoulder calm (no wide grip, no overhead pressing).  
**Loads are based on estimated TMs:** SQ **420**, BP **290**, DL **535** (adjust on the fly if RPE runs high/low).

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Squat competition pattern + bench volume (shoulder-friendly)  
**Location:** Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym (Tempe, AZ)  
**Duration:** ~100–110 minutes

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike or brisk incline walk:** 4 minutes easy
2. **90/90 hip switches:** 8/side
3. **Bodyweight squat w/ 2-sec pause in the hole:** 2 × 5 (own depth)
4. **Banded lateral walk:** 2 × 10 steps/side
5. **Shoulder prep (impingement-friendly):**
   - Band external rotation: 2 × 20/side  
   - Face pulls: 2 × 20

## Main Workout

### 1. Competition Squat (high-bar/low-bar as you compete)
**Target:** 1 × 1 @ 75% TM (RPE 6–7), then 4 × 5 @ 70% TM  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 135 × 5
  - 225 × 3
  - 275 × 2
  - 295 × 1 (warm-up, final check on depth)
- **Top single:**
  - **315 × 1** (RPE ~6.5) — meet-depth rep, clean walkout/brace
- **Back-off sets (4 × 5 @ 295):**
  - Set 1: **295 × 5** (RPE 6)
  - Set 2: **295 × 5** (RPE 6)
  - Set 3: **295 × 5** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 4: **295 × 5** (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 3–4 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Treat every rep like a judged rep: hard brace, controlled descent, and **clearly below parallel** without losing tightness.

### 2. Paused Squat (2-count in the hole)
**Target:** 3 × 3 @ 60% TM  
- **250 × 3** (RPE 6)
- **250 × 3** (RPE 6)
- **250 × 3** (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Stay tight in the bottom—don’t “relax” into the pause. Same stance/foot pressure as comp squat.

### 3. Bench Press (competition grip — avoid wide)
**Target:** 5 × 6 @ 67% TM (RPE 6–7)  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 95 × 8
  - 135 × 5
  - 165 × 3
  - 185 × 1 (warm-up)
- **Working sets (5 × 6 @ 195):**
  - Set 1: **195 × 6** (RPE 6)
  - Set 2: **195 × 6** (RPE 6)
  - Set 3: **195 × 6** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 4: **195 × 6** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 5: **195 × 6** (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Moderate grip, elbows tucked ~45–60°. Prioritize a stable touch point and consistent pause length (brief but controlled).

### 4. Chest-Supported DB Row
**Target:** 4 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **90s × 10**
- **90s × 10**
- **90s × 10**
- **90s × 10**
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Chest stays planted; pull to hip line; no shrugging.

### 5. Reverse Hyper
**Target:** 3 × 12 (smooth)  
- **180 × 12**
- **180 × 12**
- **180 × 12**
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds  
**Notes:** Controlled swing—this is traction + pump, not max effort.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Couch stretch (hip flexor):** 45 sec/side  
2. **Adductor rock-backs:** 10/side  
3. **Doorway pec stretch (gentle):** 30 sec/side  
4. **Band lat stretch:** 30 sec/side  

## Notes
- Goal achieved: low-RPE start week, lots of high-quality depth reps.
- If shoulder feels “pinchy” after bench volume, next week we’ll slightly narrow grip and/or add 1 extra warm-up set at 165–185.

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Bench intensity + deadlift speed/positioning  
**Location:** Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym  
**Duration:** ~90–100 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Row erg:** 3 minutes easy
2. **Scap push-ups:** 2 × 12
3. **Band external rotations:** 2 × 20/side
4. **Empty bar bench (pause each rep 1-sec):** 2 × 8
5. **Hip hinge patterning (RDL with empty bar):** 1 × 10

## Main Workout

### 1. Bench Press (competition pause)
**Target:** 1 × 1 @ 80% TM (RPE 7–8), then 4 × 4 @ 75%  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 95 × 8
  - 135 × 5
  - 175 × 3
  - 205 × 1 (warm-up)
  - 225 × 1 (warm-up, last feeler)
- **Top single:**
  - **235 × 1** (RPE ~7.5) — clean pause, smooth press
- **Back-off sets (4 × 4 @ 220):**
  - Set 1: **220 × 4** (RPE 7)
  - Set 2: **220 × 4** (RPE 7)
  - Set 3: **220 × 4** (RPE 7.5)
  - Set 4: **220 × 4** (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Treat the pause as a position: lats locked, wrists stacked, press “back” toward rack slightly.

### 2. Spoto Press (pause 1–2" off chest)
**Target:** 3 × 5 @ 65% TM  
- **190 × 5** (RPE 6.5)
- **190 × 5** (RPE 7)
- **190 × 5** (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes  
**Notes:** This is your sticking-point builder—hold perfectly still, then drive hard.

### 3. Deadlift (technique/speed — power bar for consistency)
**Target:** 6 × 2 @ 65% TM (fast, identical setup)  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 135 × 5
  - 225 × 3
  - 315 × 2
- **Working sets (6 × 2 @ 350):**
  - Set 1: **350 × 2** (RPE 6)
  - Set 2: **350 × 2** (RPE 6)
  - Set 3: **350 × 2** (RPE 6)
  - Set 4: **350 × 2** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 5: **350 × 2** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 6: **350 × 2** (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Wedge, brace, push the floor away. If bar speed slows, keep load and extend rest to 2 minutes.

### 4. Neutral-Grip Lat Pulldown
**Target:** 4 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **180 × 8**
- **180 × 8**
- **180 × 8**
- **180 × 8**
**Rest:** 90 seconds  
**Notes:** Think “elbows to ribs,” avoid cranky shoulder positions.

### 5. Shoulder Prehab (2 movements)
**Target:** 2 rounds  
1. **Face pulls:** 20 reps @ **50 lb**  
2. **Band external rotations:** 20/side (light band)  
**Rest:** minimal

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. **Hanging from pull-up bar (light traction):** 2 × 20–30 sec  
2. **Sleeper stretch (gentle):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Child’s pose w/ side reach (lats):** 30 sec/side  

## Notes
- Bench single should feel like “strong practice,” not a test. If shoulder nags, we’ll swap Spoto to Swiss bar next week.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Squat volume with depth + long-pause bench command practice  
**Location:** Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym  
**Duration:** ~95–110 minutes

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike:** 4 minutes easy
2. **Leg swings:** 10 front/back + 10 side/side per leg
3. **Paused bodyweight squat (2-sec):** 2 × 5
4. **Banded TKE (knee lockout):** 2 × 15/side
5. **Bench-specific warm-up:** empty bar pauses 1 × 10, then 95 × 8 (easy)

## Main Workout

### 1. Competition Squat
**Target:** 5 × 4 @ 70% TM (RPE ~6.5–7)  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 135 × 5
  - 225 × 3
  - 275 × 2
- **Working sets (5 × 4 @ 295):**
  - Set 1: **295 × 4** (RPE 6)
  - Set 2: **295 × 4** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 3: **295 × 4** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 4: **295 × 4** (RPE 7)
  - Set 5: **295 × 4** (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Same depth standard as Monday. If any rep is questionable, slow the descent slightly and own it.

### 2. Tempo Squat (3 sec down)
**Target:** 3 × 3 @ 55% TM  
- **Warm-ups:** 185 × 3 (tempo, easy)
- **Working sets:**
  - **230 × 3** (RPE 6)
  - **230 × 3** (RPE 6.5)
  - **230 × 3** (RPE 6.5)
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes  
**Notes:** Count the tempo; no dive-bombing. This is your “depth insurance policy.”

### 3. Bench Press (long pause / commands practice)
**Target:** 6 × 3 @ 70% TM (all paused)  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 95 × 8
  - 135 × 5
  - 165 × 3
  - 185 × 1 (pause)
- **Working sets (6 × 3 @ 205):**
  - Set 1: **205 × 3** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 2: **205 × 3** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 3: **205 × 3** (RPE 7)
  - Set 4: **205 × 3** (RPE 7)
  - Set 5: **205 × 3** (RPE 7)
  - Set 6: **205 × 3** (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes  
**Notes:** Practice: “start” → controlled lower → **clearly paused** → press without losing tightness.

### 4. 1-Arm Cable Row
**Target:** 3 × 12/side @ RPE 7  
- **80 × 12/side**
- **80 × 12/side**
- **80 × 12/side**
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds  
**Notes:** Slight torso rotation allowed, but keep it controlled.

### 5. Cable Crunch
**Target:** 3 × 12  
- **120 × 12**
- **120 × 12**
- **120 × 12**
**Rest:** 60 seconds  
**Notes:** Exhale hard and brace at the bottom of each rep.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Adductor stretch (side lunge hold):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Hamstring stretch (banded):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Pec minor stretch on post:** 30 sec/side  
4. **Thoracic extension over foam roller:** 6 slow reps  

## Notes
- If squat depth drifts as you fatigue, film Set 4–5 next week and we’ll adjust stance/toe angle or add a slightly longer pause.

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Deadlift intensity + bench assistance (shoulder-friendly)  
**Location:** Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym  
**Duration:** ~95–105 minutes

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Row erg:** 3 minutes easy
2. **Glute bridge:** 2 × 10
3. **Hamstring floss (band or machine light):** 1 × 15
4. **Deadlift patterning:** empty bar RDL 1 × 10
5. **Quick shoulder prep:** band pull-aparts 2 × 20

## Main Workout

### 1. Competition Deadlift (power bar)
**Target:** 1 × 1 @ 80% TM (RPE 7–8), then 4 × 3 @ 75%  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 135 × 5
  - 225 × 3
  - 315 × 2
  - 365 × 1
  - 405 × 1 (warm-up)
- **Top single:**
  - **430 × 1** (RPE ~7.5) — smooth pull, no hitch
- **Back-off sets (4 × 3 @ 400):**
  - Set 1: **400 × 3** (RPE 7)
  - Set 2: **400 × 3** (RPE 7)
  - Set 3: **400 × 3** (RPE 7.5)
  - Set 4: **400 × 3** (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 3–4 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Same start every rep: lats tight, shins close, push through midfoot. No yanking.

### 2. 1" Deficit Deadlift (off-floor builder)
**Target:** 3 × 4 @ 65% TM  
- **350 × 4** (RPE 6.5)
- **350 × 4** (RPE 7)
- **350 × 4** (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes  
**Notes:** Keep hips from shooting up—this is legs + position, not back-only.

### 3. Close-Grip Bench Press (moderate close; shoulder-comfortable)
**Target:** 4 × 6 @ 65% TM (RPE 6.5–7.5)  
- **Warm-ups:**
  - 45 × 10
  - 95 × 8
  - 135 × 5
  - 165 × 3
- **Working sets (4 × 6 @ 190):**
  - Set 1: **190 × 6** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 2: **190 × 6** (RPE 6.5)
  - Set 3: **190 × 6** (RPE 7)
  - Set 4: **190 × 6** (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2 minutes  
**Notes:** Elbows tucked, touch point slightly lower on sternum, press back toward the rack.

### 4. Rear Delt Raise (DB or machine)
**Target:** 3 × 15  
- **25s × 15**
- **25s × 15**
- **25s × 15**
**Rest:** 60 seconds  
**Notes:** Pinkies slightly up, stop before shoulder irritation.

### 5. Seated or Lying Hamstring Curl
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **150 × 12**
- **150 × 12**
- **150 × 12**
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Lat stretch (banded):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Figure-4 glute stretch:** 45 sec/side  
3. **Hamstring stretch:** 30 sec/side  
4. **Easy decompression walk:** 2 minutes

## Notes
- If grip or low back feels smoked after deadlifts, keep deficit pulls strict and reduce to 2 sets next week—quality > fatigue this early.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Squat:** Strong technical week at conservative loads (Top singles **315**, volume anchored at **295**; paused **250** and tempo **230** reinforcing meet depth).
- **Bench:** 3 exposures hit the intent—volume (195×6s), intensity (single **235** + 220×4s), and command practice (205×3 long pauses). Spoto work directly targeted the **2–3" off-chest** zone.
- **Deadlift:** Good structure: speed work **350×2** and heavy day single **430** with solid back-offs **400×3**; deficit pulls added off-floor strength without overreaching.
- **Constraint monitoring (right shoulder impingement):** Shoulder-friendly pressing only (no wide grip/OHP). Prehab included 2–3x this week—keep daily light band work.
- **Weak points:** Depth consistency addressed with paused/tempo squats; bench mid-range addressed with Spoto + long pauses—on track for Phase 1 goals.
- **Next week:** Move to **Week 2** progression (+2.5% on key back-offs if RPE stayed ≤7.5), or hold loads if shoulder or fatigue trends up. If you tell me whether you’ll **use a Texas deadlift bar in the meet**, we’ll standardize bar choice starting Week 2.</Week>
